title: Dreamy Buttery Irish Chinese Cabbage Stew
description: A comforting, slow-cooked stew featuring Chinese cabbage, perfect for a sick day, wrapped in a ti leaf for added flavor and nutrition. This dish is anti-inflammatory friendly and features the richness of butter.
instructions: In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped Chinese cabbage and cook until it is wilted, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped potatoes, chicken broth, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Prepare the ti leaves by washing and drying them, then cutting off the stems.
To serve, place a portion of the stew in the center of each ti leaf, then fold the leaves over the stew to form a packet.
Serve the packets hot, with additional melted butter on the side if desired.
